title: Architecture Evaluation, Quality Attributes, and Decision Governance · Claim Defense
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for architecture evaluation, quality attributes, and decision governance and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for evaluated architecture and decision governance packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Describe context, containers, components, data, deployment, runtime, trust, operations, and ownership views at useful levels.
Analyze concrete quality-attribute scenarios and trade accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, cost, and simplicity explicitly.
Define failure domains, resource bounds, dependency timeouts, degraded modes, safe stop, recovery, and blast-radius evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Architecture Review
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Architecture Review: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A box diagram omits quality tradeoffs, trust and failure boundaries, cost, evolution, dissent, review triggers, and rejected alternatives. The quiz variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Run a structured review with scenarios, alternatives, evidence gaps, dissent, decisions, owners, and timed re-evaluation.

Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Architecture Review.
steps.why: Retrieving Architecture Review through the concrete evaluated architecture and decision governance packet case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Architecture Review, keep Architecture Review connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Run a structured review with scenarios, alternatives, evidence gaps, dissent, decisions, owners, and timed re-evaluation.
